blocks.body: Present Perfect Continuous: Questions and Short Answers — Have you been...?, Has she been...?, How long have they been...?

{"level":"A2–B1","intro":"In questions with Present Perfect Continuous, we put have / has before the subject and then use been + V-ing.","rules":[{"label":"Have I / you / we / they been ...?","color":"emerald","text":"With these subjects, start the question with have.","example":"Have you been waiting long?"},{"label":"Has he / she / it been ...?","color":"blue","text":"With he / she / it, use has.","example":"Has she been working today?"},{"label":"How long ...?","color":"amber","text":"Questions with How long are very common when we talk about duration up to now.","example":"How long have they been living here?"},{"label":"Short answers","color":"violet","text":"In a short answer, repeat have / has or haven't / hasn't.","example":"Yes, I have. / No, she hasn't."}]}

{"title":"1. Basic question models","intro":"In a question, have or has comes first, then been + V-ing.","items":[{"label":"I / you / we / they","title":"Have ... been + V-ing?","subtitle":"Have you been studying? / Have they been waiting long?"},{"label":"he / she / it","title":"Has ... been + V-ing?","subtitle":"Has he been working? / Has it been raining?"},{"label":"How long","title":"How long have / has ... been + V-ing?","subtitle":"How long have you been learning English?"},{"label":"Short answers","title":"Yes, ... have / has. No, ... haven't / hasn't.","subtitle":"Yes, I have. / No, he hasn't."}]}

{"title":"2. What these questions are for","sections":[{"label":"About duration","color":"emerald","description":"Ask how long an action has continued up to the present moment.","examples":[{"en":"How long have you been waiting?","ua":"A question about duration."},{"en":"How long has she been working here?","ua":"A period up to now."}]},{"label":"About present or recent activity","color":"blue","description":"Ask whether someone has been in a process recently or now.","examples":[{"en":"Have you been studying all morning?","ua":"The process may still be continuing."},{"en":"Has he been sleeping well lately?","ua":"A recent repeated situation."}]},{"label":"About a visible result","color":"amber","description":"These questions often explain a present result through a recent process.","examples":[{"en":"Why are you tired? Have you been running?","ua":"A recent process explains the result now."},{"en":"Has she been crying?","ua":"We ask because we can see something now."}]}]}

{"title":"3. Question and short answer","intro":"In the answer, repeat have / has, not the whole sentence.","rows":[{"en":"Have you been waiting long?","ua":"Yes, I have. / No, I haven't.","note":"have → have / haven't"},{"en":"Has she been working today?","ua":"Yes, she has. / No, she hasn't.","note":"has → has / hasn't"},{"en":"How long have they been living here?","ua":"For two years.","note":"a question about duration"}],"warning":"Do not say Has she been work? or Yes, she have."}

{"title":"4. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Putting have / has after the subject instead of before it.","wrong":"❌ She has been working today?","right":"✅ Has she been working today?"},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Leaving out been in the question.","wrong":"❌ Have you studying long?","right":"✅ Have you been studying long?"},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Building How long with the wrong word order.","wrong":"❌ How long you have been waiting?","right":"✅ How long have you been waiting?"}]}

{"title":"5. Short summary","items":["Questions in Present Perfect Continuous = Have / Has + subject + been + V-ing.","Use have with I / you / we / they.","Use has with he / she / it.","How long is very common for duration up to now.","In short answers, repeat have / has or haven't / hasn't."]}
